prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, dear, a CPU is something they call the "brain" of the computer. It’s a little chip inside the computer that helps it think and do all the things you ask it to do, like play games or write letters. You know how I follow a recipe when I bake cookies? The CPU follows instructions, too, but much, much faster. I don’t know all the fancy details, but I do know that without the CPU, the computer just wouldn’t work at all. It’s small, but very important—kind of like the conductor of an orchestra, making sure everything happens at the right time.
